comments.body:
Need assistance from L3 team:

Case: Signature option not showing in MCSL SLGP

Details:
A $200 Custom Plan MCSL customer recently migrated to the FedEx REST API and reported that the signature option is not showing on the SLGP screen.

With FedEx SOAP and other carriers, we are able to see the “Signature Required” checkbox on the SLGP screen. However, for FedEx REST, the option is not appearing.

We were also able to replicate the same behavior in the support-qa collabo QA MCSL app.

Could you please check and provide your input?

Store Details:
Store URL - rugged-race-products.myshopify.com
Account ID - ec7136eb-95e8-437b-a415-b61d2fc684e9

 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/E2CtZuUDVC0UBToEQMr8q6TC4/?name=image.png) ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/iwDs78RjFDVeMbnruFunnWNFr/?name=image.png)
comments.html_body:

With FedEx SOAP and other carriers, we are able to see the “Signature Required” checkbox on the SLGP screen. However, for FedEx REST, the option is not appearing.

comments.body:
Hello Reggie,

Thank you for your time on the call.

As discussed on the call, the app settings have been reverted to the old FedEx account. I also reviewed this with Manohar, who had connected with you regarding the account migration.

**Regarding the rate difference** between the new and old FedEx accounts, it was related to the ONE RATE option, which in turn is associated with the packaging configuration. Once I disabled the ONE RATE option in the new FedEx account, I was able to match the rates with the old account. _(I had tested this during our call)_

As of now, we will not be making any further changes from our end, and **the store has been restored to the same setup as before the new integration.**

Additionally, the orders highlighted in the video below appear to have been placed while the FedEx REST account was enabled. That is why the carrier name for these orders appears as “FedEx REST.” Reprocessing these orders will reassign them to the old account.
Video Recording : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1whjocbB0_LpaQ-mlxSXaQLxk4LYsyeZC/view?usp=sharing

To reprocess the orders:
- Select the orders
- Click on **More Actions**
- Choose **Reprocess Orders**

Since there was previously a rate mismatch between the old and new accounts, I have not made any changes to these orders from my end as of now. Please let us know if you would like us to reassign these orders to the old account.

We can reconnect next week at your convenience to discuss further regarding the migration. We will keep you posted.

Best regards,
Keerthi
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Reggie, 
 
Thank you for your time on the call.
 
As discussed on the call, the app settings have been reverted to the old FedEx account. I also reviewed this with Manohar, who had connected with you regarding the account migration.
 
Regarding the rate difference between the new and old FedEx accounts, it was related to the ONE RATE option, which in turn is associated with the packaging configuration. Once I disabled the ONE RATE option in the new FedEx account, I was able to match the rates with the old account. (I had tested this during our call)
 
As of now, we will not be making any further changes from our end, and the store has been restored to the same setup as before the new integration.
 
Additionally, the orders highlighted in the video below appear to have been placed while the FedEx REST account was enabled. That is why the carrier name for these orders appears as “FedEx REST.” Reprocessing these orders will reassign them to the old account.
Video Recording : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1whjocbB0_LpaQ-mlxSXaQLxk4LYsyeZC/view?usp=sharing
 
To reprocess the orders:
  • Select the orders
  • Click on More Actions

  • Choose Reprocess Orders

     

Since there was previously a rate mismatch between the old and new accounts, I have not made any changes to these orders from my end as of now. Please let us know if you would like us to reassign these orders to the old account.
 
We can reconnect next week at your convenience to discuss further regarding the migration. We will keep you posted.
 
Best regards,
Keerthi
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Reggie,

Thank you for your time over the call.

To recap, the FedEx REST account is active, and the shipping rates displayed at checkout are being fetched from the FedEx REST account.

Please note that the old FedEx account is still active only under the Label Automation rules to allow your team to process all pending orders. Once all pending orders have been processed, the old FedEx account can be disabled. I have attached a short video demonstrating how this can be done.

Regarding the fallback rates case, I would like to inform you that there was a temporary outage on May 27, 2026, which was addressed promptly.

Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need any assistance or additional information from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

Regarding the fallback rates case, I would like to inform you that there was a temporary outage on May 27, 2026, which was addressed promptly.
 
Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need any assistance or additional information from our end.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
**Call Summary:**

- The FedEx REST account has been activated, and both shipping rates and label generation are working successfully through the FedEx REST account.
- Shipping rates were verified and confirmed to be accurate and matching as expected.
- The customer confirmed that label generation using the FedEx REST account is functioning properly.
- The old FedEx account is still active to allow processing of pending orders. The customer to deactivate the old account once all pending orders are completed, likely this week.


Call recording - https://drive.google.com/file/d/1tMWp62Wwlqvm7-Jt7A1uhECbiFMcyf8B/view?usp=sharing
